title: 10 best herbal creams for puffy eyes

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Adiantum contains flavonoids and saponins that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce puffiness and swelling under the eyes.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Adiantum, such as vitamin C and E, help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and contribute to eye puffiness.
Vasoconstrictive properties: Adiantum's ability to constrict blood vessels can help reduce fluid accumulation under the eyes, leading to a reduction in puffiness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with puffy eyes because it contains antioxidants and flavonoids that reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the delicate skin around the eyes. This helps to decrease puffiness and dark circles, leaving the area looking smoother and more refreshed. The anti-inflammatory properties of Ginkgo biloba also help to reduce swelling and promote a more even skin tone, giving the appearance of brighter, healthier-looking eyes.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These powerful antioxidants help reduce inflammation and swelling around the eyes, thereby minimizing puffiness.
Bilobalide: This triterpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce fluid retention and edema around the eyes, resulting in less puffiness.
Quercetin: As a potent flavonoid and antioxidant, quercetin helps to reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and break down excess fluid that contributes to puffy eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties to reduce puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.
Seeds: Used for their flavonoid content, which helps to improve circulation and reduce swelling in the delicate skin around the eyes.
Barks: Used for their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties to help tighten and soothe the skin around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves in early fall when they turn bright yellow and have a strong earthy scent.
Dry the leaves in a warm place for 1-2 weeks to reduce moisture content and preserve them.
Steep 2 tablespoons of dried ginkgo biloba leaves in 1 cup of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at room temperature for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer the infused oil to a clean container.
Blend 2 tablespoons of the infused oil with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of shea butter to create a smooth creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Caffeine: Constricts blood vessels, reducing puffiness by minimizing water retention and swelling.
Menthol: Cools and decongests the skin, reducing inflammation and easing tension around the eyes to reduce puffiness.
Vitamin K: Helps to reduce dark circles and discoloration under the eyes by promoting collagen production and improving circulation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with puffy eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol in peppermint oil cools and constricts blood vessels, reducing swelling and fluid accumulation under the eyes. Additionally, peppermint's invigorating aroma can help to reduce stress and promote a sense of relaxation, which can also contribute to minimizing puffiness. By applying peppermint cream to the affected area, individuals can experience a refreshing and rejuvenating effect that leaves their eyes looking and feeling more refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound with anti-inflammatory properties that helps reduce swelling and puffiness in the eyes.
Menthol: A terpene with vasoconstrictive properties that helps reduce blood flow to the eye area, thus reducing puffiness and swelling.
Menthone: A terpene that has a cooling effect on the skin, which can help constrict blood vessels and reduce puffiness in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are commonly used due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which provide a cooling effect that helps to reduce puffiness.
Flowers: They are often used because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate eye puffiness.
Roots: They are typically used as they contain a higher concentration of menthol and menthone compared to leaves, making them more effective in reducing puffiness and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves when the plant is in full bloom for optimal oil content.
Steam distill 1/2 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves for 2 hours to obtain the essential oil.
Mix 1 tablespoon of the essential oil with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of beeswax.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 10 minutes to melt the beeswax and emulsify the ingredients.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes before use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: The menthol and cineole present in Tasmanian Blue Gum have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and puffiness around the eyes.

Decongestant property: The eucalyptol in Tasmanian Blue Gum has a natural decongestant effect, which can help shrink blood vessels and reduce fluid accumulation under the eyes, leading to reduced puffiness.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidant compounds present in Tasmanian Blue Gum, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help neutralize free radicals and protect the delicate skin around the eyes from oxidative stress and damage.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus, also known as Tasmanian blue gum, creams helps with puffy eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties and ability to improve circulation. The eucalyptus oil present in these creams contains compounds that help reduce swelling and ease puffiness, making it an effective treatment for tired, puffy eyes. Additionally, the cooling sensation of eucalyptus globulus helps to constrict blood vessels, further reducing the appearance of puffiness and leaving the skin looking refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): Cineole helps reduce puffiness by constricting blood vessels and reducing inflammation in the affected area.
Limonene: Limonene acts as a natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce swelling and ease congestion associated with puffy eyes.
Tannins: Tannins help reduce puffiness by constricting blood vessels and decreasing fluid retention, which can contribute to swollen eyelids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high eucalyptol content, which is a natural decongestant that helps reduce puffiness and inflammation.
Barks: They are used due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm the skin around puffy eyes.
Stems: They are used due to their rich content of flavonoids and tannins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce puffiness and promote skin health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest eucalyptus globulus leaves in large quantities from mature plants to ensure high-quality essential oil.
Dry the harvested leaves in a well-ventilated area for about 2 weeks to achieve optimal moisture content.
Steep 500 grams of dried leaves in 5 liters of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth to remove the solids and discard the solids.
Mix 100 milliliters of the infused oil with 100 grams of beeswax and 100 milliliters of distilled water to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold contains flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling, making it effective in alleviating puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold cream help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to puffy eyes.
Vasodilatory properties: Pot marigold's ability to dilate blood vessels can help reduce fluid buildup under the eyes, which is often a cause of puffiness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with puffy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The flavonoids and carotenoids present in Calendula officinalis have been shown to reduce swelling and improve circulation, which in turn helps to reduce puffiness. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to hydrate the delicate skin around the eyes, leaving it feeling smooth and refreshed. Regular use of Calendula officinalis creams can help to reduce the appearance of puffy eyes and leave the skin looking more youthful and radiant.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce puffiness and swelling around the eyes by suppressing the release of inflammatory mediators.
Triterpenes (e.g., oleanolic acid and ursolic acid): These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help alleviate puffiness and promote healthy skin around the eyes.

Flavonoids (e.g., quercetin and kaempferol): These compounds have strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce puffiness and protect the delicate skin around the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce puffiness and soothe the skin around the eyes.
Leaves: They are used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and calm irritated skin around the eyes.
Seeds: They are used for their emollient and soothing properties, which help to moisturize and calm the skin around the eyes, reducing puffiness and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers in full bloom during peak sun hours for optimal potency and fragrance.
Dry the harvested flowers in a low-temperature dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 8 hours.
Steep 2 ounces of dried flowers in 4 cups of carrier oil such as coconut oil at room temperature for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then add 2 tablespoons of beeswax.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 160 degrees fahrenheit for 15 minutes to create a smooth creamy consistency.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile cream's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce puffiness by reducing swelling and soothing irritated skin around the eyes.
Antioxidant property: Chamomile cream's antioxidant property helps neutralize free radicals, which can cause eye puffiness and dark circles, promoting a smoother and more even-toned appearance.

Soothing and calming property: Chamomile cream's soothing and calming properties help to reduce redness and irritation, making it easier for the skin around the eyes to relax and recover from puffiness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with puffy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The active compounds in chamomile, such as apigenin and luteolin, have been shown to reduce swelling and ease irritation. By applying a chamomile cream to the affected area, the skin's natural ability to repair itself is enhanced, and the appearance of puffiness is minimized. This natural remedy provides a gentle and effective solution for reducing puffy eyes and promoting a more refreshed and revitalized look.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: An apigenin is a flavonoid that helps with puffy eyes by reducing inflammation and acting as a natural anti-edematous agent.
Chamazulene: Chamazulene is an isobezoic lactone that helps with puffy eyes by reducing inflammation, acting as an anti-allergic and anti-inflammatory agent.

Matricine: Matricine is a sesquiterpene lactone that helps with puffy eyes by reducing inflammation, acting as an anti-allergic and anti-inflammatory agent, and also by its ability to relax muscles and improve lymphatic drainage.
remedy_parts: Flowers: These are the primary source of chamazulene and apigenin, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help reduce puffiness and calm the skin.

Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, the leaves of Matricaria chamomilla contribute to the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of the creams, helping to soothe and calm the skin around the eyes.

Seeds: Although less commonly used, the seeds of Matricaria chamomilla are sometimes incorporated into eye creams for their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce puffiness and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers at dawn when their potency is highest and dry them completely in a low-temperature oven.
Combine 100g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the jar daily to ensure even infusion.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the infused oil from the plant material.
Mix 100g of the infused oil with 50g of beeswax and 20g of emulsifying wax in a double boiler to create a stable cream base.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Many herbal teas, such as green tea and chamomile, contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce puffiness by soothing and calming the skin.

Constricting and decongesting properties: Herbal teas like peppermint and eucalyptus have a cooling effect on the skin, which helps to constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid buildup, thereby reducing puffiness.

Anti-allergic and anti-inflammatory properties: Herbs like calendula and lavender have anti-allergic and anti-inflammatory effects that help alleviate inflammation and itchiness associated with puffy eyes, promoting a smoother and more even complexion.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis, also known as tea, creams helps with puffy eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The camellia extract in these creams is rich in catechins and theaflavins, which help to reduce swelling and soothe the delicate skin around the eyes. As a result, the appearance of dark circles and puffiness is reduced, leaving the eyes looking refreshed and revitalized. The cooling sensation of the cream can also help to de-puff the eyes, making it an effective remedy for tired and puffy eyes.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: Catechins, particularly epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), help reduce puffiness by constricting blood vessels and preventing fluid accumulation.
Quercetin: Quercetin, a flavonoid, acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and alleviating puffiness by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Theaflavins: Theaflavins, a type of flavonoid, possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the skin around the eyes, reducing puffiness and promoting a more refreshed appearance.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce puffiness and calm the skin.
Stems: Utilized for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and promote skin health.
Buds: Employed for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce puffiness and soothe the skin around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500 grams of dried camellia sinensis leaves and place them in a heat-proof container.
Steam the camellia sinensis leaves with 2 liters of water at 100 degrees celsius for 30 minutes.
Combine 300 grams of base oil such as sweet almond oil or grapeseed oil with 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Add 50 grams of the camellia sinensis infusion to the base oil mixture and stir until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into a heat-proof mold and allow it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help reduce puffiness by constricting blood vessels and reducing swelling.
Antioxidant properties: Lavender's antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, neutralize free radicals that can contribute to puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.
Sedative and calming properties: Lavender's calming effects can help reduce stress and promote relaxation, which can lead to a reduction in puffiness caused by fatigue, tension, or anxiety.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with puffy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The calming effects of the lavender oil can reduce puffiness by constricting blood vessels and reducing fluid retention. Additionally, the antiseptic and antibacterial properties of the lavender oil help to eliminate bacteria and other foreign particles that may be causing puffiness. remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene that helps reduce puffiness by promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation in the skin and underlying tissues.
Luteolin: A phenolic compound that exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and soothe irritated skin around the eyes.
Lavandulol: A terpene that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate puffiness and promote a more even skin tone around the eyes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used for their calming and anti-inflammatory properties to soothe puffy eyes.
Leaves: Used for their cooling effect and ability to reduce puffiness and inflammation around the eyes.
Seeds: Used for their antioxidant properties and ability to reduce swelling and promote relaxation, which can help alleviate puffy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at the peak of blooming for maximum potency retention.
Dry the flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Combine 30g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 500ml of carrier oil in a double boiler setup.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 hours under low heat to allow the flowers to infuse their essence.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and bottle the infused oil to be used in cream formulation.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps reduce puffiness by inhibiting the release of histamine, a chemical that causes blood vessels to dilate and lead to swelling.
Flavonoids: Act as powerful antioxidants that combat oxidative stress and inflammation in the skin, which can contribute to puffy eyes.
Saponins: Have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated skin around the eyes, reducing redness and puffiness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with puffy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The root of the Glycyrrhiza glabra plant contains compounds that reduce swelling and soothe irritated skin. These creams work by constricting blood vessels and decreasing water retention, which helps to reduce puffiness and discoloration around the eyes. As a result, they provide a more refreshed and rejuvenated appearance, making them a popular choice for those looking to reduce the appearance of puffy eyes.
remedy_constituents: Licorice Saponins: These triterpenoid saponins help reduce inflammation and edema, which contribute to puffy eyes, by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and puffiness by inhibiting the activity of certain enzymes involved in inflammation.

Flavonoids: These compounds, particularly isoflavones and flavanones, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to puffy eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, which helps reduce inflammation and puffiness by regulating fluid retention.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids and other compounds that contribute to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Barks: Used for their emollient and anti-inflammatory properties, which help calm and reduce puffiness.
remedy_recipe: Extract 200 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots with a solvent like ethanol or glycerin for 2 hours.
Filter the liquid extract through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove solid particles.
Combine the filtered extract with 100 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture under low heat for 30 minutes to melt the beeswax and mix well.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for at least 2 hours before packaging the final cream product.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal Siberian Ginseng cream's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can cause puffiness and inflammation in the delicate skin around the eyes.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Siberian Ginseng cream reduce swelling and redness caused by allergens, stress, or fluid retention, helping to alleviate puffy eyes.
Vasoconstrictive properties: Herbal Siberian Ginseng's vasoconstrictive properties help tighten blood vessels around the eyes, reducing excess fluid buildup and puffiness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eleutherococcus senticosus, also known as Siberian ginseng, creams helps with puffy eyes because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and soothe irritated skin. The cream's cooling sensation can help to depuff the eyes by increasing blood flow and reducing water retention. Additionally, Eleutherococcus senticosus has antioxidant properties that protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, promoting a smoother and more even appearance. This can lead to a more refreshed and revitalized look.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Eleutherococcus senticosus creams help with puffy eyes by reducing inflammation and improving circulation, which can help to alleviate swelling and discoloration.

Phenolic acids (including ferulic acid and caffeic acid): Phenolic acids in Eleutherococcus senticosus creams help with puffy eyes by exhibiting antioxidant properties, which can help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress that can contribute to puffiness.

Eleutherosides (specifically eleutheroside B): Eleutherosides in Eleutherococcus senticosus creams help with puffy eyes by acting as anti-inflammatory agents, which can help to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with puffy eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain eleutherosides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce puffiness and swelling.
Barks: They contain eleutherosides and other compounds that help to relax and soothe the skin around the eyes, reducing puffiness and dark circles.
Seeds: They contain antioxidants and other beneficial compounds that help to protect and nourish the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing puffiness and promoting healthy skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-50 grams of dried eleutherococcus senticosus roots from a trusted supplier or grow your own.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 30 grams of the powder with 60 grams of beeswax and 30 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30-40 minutes or until it reaches 160-180 degrees fahrenheit.
Strain and whip the mixture until it thickens and emulsifies into a smooth cream consistency.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Marshmallow root contains mucilages that help reduce inflammation and swelling around the eye area, relieving puffiness and discomfort.

Moisturizing: The emollient properties of marshmallow root cream hydrate and soothe dry skin, reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles and leaving the under-eye area looking smoother and more refreshed.

Antioxidant: Marshmallow root is rich in antioxidants that help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from environmental stressors and free radicals, which can contribute to puffiness and dark circles.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis, also known as marshmallow, creams helps with puffy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The root of the marshmallow plant contains mucilages, which are gel-like substances that help to calm and reduce swelling. When applied to the skin around the eyes, these mucilages work to depuff the area, reducing the appearance of dark circles and bags. The cream's ability to lock in moisture also helps to hydrate and nourish the delicate skin around the eyes, leaving it feeling soft and refreshed.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly kaempferol and quercetin, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce puffiness and promote healthy blood vessels around the eyes.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used because they contain high amounts of mucilage, a soothing and hydrating agent that helps to reduce puffiness and inflammation around the eyes.

Flowers: The flowers are used because they contain flavonoids and other antioxidants that help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the skin around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried althaea officinalis root and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground root powder with 100g of sweet almond oil and 20g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes, then remove from heat and stir until the beeswax is fully incorporated.
Add 10 drops of vitamin e oil and 10 drops of lavender essential oil to the mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and allow it to cool and set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock creams helps with puffy eyes because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and soothe irritation. The cream's natural ingredients, such as arctin and inulin, work to calm the delicate skin around the eyes, diminishing puffiness and dark circles. Additionally, burdock root has been shown to improve circulation, which can help to reduce fluid retention and alleviate puffy eyes caused by fatigue, stress, or allergies. By using an herbal burdock cream, individuals can enjoy a more radiant and rested appearance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Burdock root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and saponins, which help reduce puffiness and swelling around the eyes by reducing inflammation.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in burdock cream, such as polyphenols and phenolic acids, help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to puffy eyes.

Anti-edema properties: Burdock root has been traditionally used to reduce edema (swelling) and fluid retention, making it effective in helping to alleviate puffy eyes by reducing excess water buildup under the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce puffiness and swelling around the eyes.
Seeds: Burdock seeds contain fatty acids and antioxidants that soothe and calm irritated skin, making them ideal for eye cream formulations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality arctium lappa roots and dried them in the shade for two weeks.
Grind 100 grams of dried root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for five minutes.
Combine the powdered root with 200 milliliters of sweet almond oil and stir until well combined for ten minutes.
Heat the mixture to 60 degrees celsius and maintain temperature for one hour and fifteen minutes.
Strain the cream through a cheesecloth and bottle immediately, using within six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with puffy eyes because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and soothe irritated skin. The antioxidants present in dandelion also help to neutralize free radicals, which can cause puffiness and dark circles under the eyes. Additionally, dandelion creams are rich in vitamins A, C, and E, which promote collagen production and improve skin elasticity, leaving the delicate skin around the eyes looking smoother and less puffy. This natural approach can provide long-lasting relief from puffy eyes without harsh chemicals or artificial fragrances.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxeryl acetate, help reduce puffiness and swelling around the eyes by minimizing inflammation.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidant properties of dandelion, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to puffiness and dark circles.

Diuretic property: Dandelion's natural diuretic effects can help reduce fluid retention in the body, which is often a contributing factor to puffy eyes, by increasing urine production and promoting the elimination of excess fluids.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective at reducing puffiness and soothing irritated skin.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that help to calm and constrict blood vessels, reducing the appearance of dark circles under the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry taraxacum officinale leaves in shade for at least two weeks or until crispy.
Grind dried taraxacum officinale leaves into fine powder using mortar and pestle for about 10 minutes.
Combine powdered taraxacum officinale with 20% glycerin and 80% coconut oil in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Heat mixture over low heat for approximately 30 minutes or until it reaches a temperature of 160 degrees fahrenheit.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature before transferring it to airtight containers for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet briar creams helps with puffy eyes because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and soothe irritated skin. The herbal extract in sweet briar cream constricts blood vessels, diminishing the appearance of dark circles and puffiness. Additionally, its antioxidant-rich ingredients neutralize free radicals, protecting the delicate skin around the eyes from environmental stressors. As a result, sweet briar creams effectively alleviate puffy eyes, leaving them feeling smoother, refreshed, and rejuvenated.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal sweet briar creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and puffiness under the eyes, providing relief from tired-looking eyes.
Antioxidants: Sweet briar's antioxidants combat free radicals that can contribute to skin damage and discoloration around the eyes, resulting in a more youthful and radiant appearance.

Tissue-regenerating properties: The cream's tissue-regenerating properties help stimulate collagen production, improving the skin's elasticity and firmness, which can reduce the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles under the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the sweet briar plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and ease puffiness in the eyes.
Barks: The barks of the sweet briar plant have astringent properties that can constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid accumulation, alleviating puffy eye symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh rosa rubiginosa petals and clean them thoroughly under cold running water.
Combine the cleaned petals with 500ml of boiling distilled water and let it steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bowl discarding the solids.
Mix 100g of beeswax pellets, 50g of coconut oil, and 25g of shea butter with the herbal infusion in a double boiler.
Whisk continuously for 10-15 minutes until the mixture thickens and emulsifies forming a smooth creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cucumber creams helps with puffy eyes because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and puffiness. The cooling effect of cucumber also constricts blood vessels, decreasing fluid buildup under the eyes. Additionally, herbal extracts in the cream, such as aloe vera and chamomile, soothe and calm the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing redness and irritation. As a result, cucumber creams provide effective relief from puffy eyes, leaving you with a more refreshed and rejuvenated appearance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal cucumber creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like cucurbitacins, which help to reduce swelling and ease puffiness around the eyes by constricting blood vessels and reducing fluid accumulation.
Antioxidants: Cucumber's antioxidant properties help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate puffy eyes and dark circles.

Hydrating agents: Herbal cucumber creams often contain humectant ingredients that draw moisture into the skin, helping to plump up the under-eye area and reduce the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles while also soothing and calming irritated skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm puffy eyes.
Seeds: Containing omega-3 fatty acids and flavonoids, they aid in reducing swelling and promoting healthy skin around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Grind 2 tablespoons of dried cucumis sativus leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Combine the powdered cucumis sativus with 4 ounces of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and stir well for 5 minutes.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 100°f to 110°f for 10 minutes, allowing the herbal extract to infuse into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids and leaving behind 3 ounces of creamy herbal oil.
Whip 2 tablespoons of beeswax with 1 tablespoon of coconut cream until smooth and well combined, then add in the cooled herbal oil and whip until a rich and luxurious cream forms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with puffy eyes because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation and swelling. The herb's natural anti-inflammatory properties help to constrict blood vessels, reducing fluid accumulation under the eyes that can cause puffiness. Additionally, fennel's antioxidant compounds help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to eye puffiness. As a result, using an herbal fennel cream on puffy eyes can provide quick and effective relief from this common concern, leaving the skin looking smoother and more refreshed.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Fennel's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and puffiness by soothing and calming irritated skin around the eyes, thereby alleviating discomfort and promoting a smooth appearance.
Antioxidants: Fennel's rich antioxidant content helps neutralize free radicals that can contribute to puffy eyes by breaking down collagen and elastin, leading to reduced firmness and elasticity.

Cicatrizant properties: Fennel's cicatrizant properties promote tissue repair and regeneration around the eyes, which can help reduce puffiness by strengthening skin elasticity and firmness, allowing it to snap back into place more easily.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce puffiness and swelling around the eyes.
Buds: Fennel buds contain flavonoids that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making them suitable for soothing and calming irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean foeniculum vulgare leaves, drying them for at least 24 hours in a warm place.
Combine 20 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil.
Steep the mixture for 4-6 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the container daily to ensure even infusion.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.
Mix 50 milliliters of the infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax and heat the mixture in a double boiler for 10-15 minutes until smooth and creamy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with puffy eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. The natural compounds in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, work to constrict blood vessels and soothe the delicate skin around the eyes. This results in a noticeable decrease in puffiness and dark circles, leaving the under-eye area looking smoother and more refreshed. Additionally, thyme creams often contain moisturizing agents that hydrate the skin, reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles for a more radiant and youthful appearance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme contains compounds like thymol, which helps reduce swelling and inflammation around the eyes, making it an effective remedy for puffy eyes.

Antioxidant properties: Thyme's antioxidant properties help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, reducing puffiness and discoloration.
Decongestant properties: Thyme's decongestant properties can help reduce fluid retention and alleviate puffiness in the eyes by constricting blood vessels and reducing swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and swelling, making them an effective ingredient in creams to alleviate puffy eyes.
Buds: Thyme buds are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm irritated eye areas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thyme leaves at dawn or early morning when essential oils are most potent for maximum benefits.
Dry the thyme leaves in a warm oven at 40°c for 24 hours to preserve their potency and aroma.
Combine one part dried thyme leaves with two parts coconut oil and heat gently over low heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar, discarding solids after 20 minutes of steeping time.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for three hours before applying a thin layer under the eyes twice daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with puffy eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which work to reduce swelling and soothe irritated skin. The cream's antioxidant compounds help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to puffiness, while also promoting healthy circulation and lymphatic drainage. Additionally, sage's astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid accumulation under the eyes, leaving the area looking smoother and more radiant.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, reduce puffiness by decreasing swelling and inflammation in the delicate skin around the eyes.

Antioxidant: Sage's antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, neutralize free radicals that can cause damage to eye tissue and contribute to puffiness, while also promoting collagen production and healthy skin elasticity.
Vasoconstrictor: Sage's essential oils, particularly camphor and borneol, help to constrict blood vessels in the eyes, reducing fluid accumulation and swelling that can cause puffiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, sage roots help reduce puffiness and soothe tired eyes.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, sage barks provide anti-edema properties that alleviate eye swelling and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried salvia officinalis leaves measuring 2 tablespoons from a trusted herbalist or store.
Combine the dried leaves with 4 ounces of sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar for infusion.
Allow the mixture to steep for 2 hours and then strain it using cheesecloth for separation.
Mix the infused oil with 8 ounces of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, and 2 teaspoons of vitamin e oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at low heat until the wax melts completely taking approximately 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with puffy eyes because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation and swelling. The active compounds in yarrow, such as achillein and chamazulene, have anti-inflammatory properties that help to constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid retention around the eye area. This natural cream also contains antioxidants that combat free radicals and promote healthy skin. As a result, yarrow creams effectively alleviate puffiness, dark circles, and bags under the eyes, leaving the skin looking smoother and more radiant.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Yarrow cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce puffiness by reducing swelling and redness around the eyes, allowing for a more even and smooth appearance.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in yarrow cream work to neutralize free radicals that can cause damage to skin cells, helping to alleviate discoloration and dark circles under the eyes.
Stimulatory property: Yarrow's stimulatory compounds help improve blood circulation around the eyes, which can reduce puffiness by removing excess fluid and promoting a smoother appearance.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and ease puffiness around the eyes.
Flowers: The flowers contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that soothe and calm the delicate skin around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers then grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground achillea millefolium powder with 100ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a small saucepan heating it over low heat for 5 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it steep at room temperature for 4 hours or overnight allowing the herbs to infuse into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter discarding the solids then add 10ml of beeswax and 5ml of vitamin e oil stirring until melted.
Pour the cream mixture into small sterilized jars filling them to the top and allowing them to cool and set at room temperature for 30 minutes before use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for puffy eyes are topical creams or ointments infused with herbal extracts, designed to reduce puffiness and alleviate discomfort around the eyes. These creams work by soothing and calming the skin, thereby reducing swelling and inflammation. The best herbal creams for puffy eyes include Aloe barbadensis, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Cucumus sativus, Calendula officinalis, Ginkgo biloba, Hamamelis virginiana, Aloe vera, Euphrasia officinalis, Camellia sinensis, and Echinacea purpurea. If left untreated, puffy eyes can lead to dark circles, fatigue, and decreased productivity, affecting one's ability to work and interact with others, ultimately impacting their quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to puffy eyes that are treated with herbal creams are dark circles, fine lines and wrinkles, and skin discoloration. Herbal creams containing ingredients like caffeine, peptides, and antioxidants help reduce puffiness by constricting blood vessels, stimulating collagen production, and neutralizing free radicals. They also soothe and moisturize the skin, leaving it looking smoother and more radiant. Examples of herbal creams for these issues include those made with chamomile, green tea, and gotu kola.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with puffy eyes is a blend of peppermint, chamomile, and aloe vera. Peppermint reduces swelling and cools the skin, while chamomile calms and soothes irritated skin. Aloe vera, with its anti-inflammatory properties, helps to reduce puffiness and hydrate the delicate skin around the eyes. Applying a mixture of these creams in a gentle circular motion can provide quick relief from puffy eyes, promoting a refreshed and rejuvenated appearance.
